mission.title: kubevirt: virtctl command not working on Arm Mac
mission.description: virtctl command not working on Arm Mac. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubevirt troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubevirt deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt -n kubevirt --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
**What happened**:

I followed [the documentation to install virtctl](https://kubevirt.io/quickstart_cloud/).

steps.title: Apply the fix for virtctl command not working on Arm Mac
steps.description: This PR adds the necessary code to build and release `virtctl` for the `darwin-arm64` architecture for Macs with Apple Silicon. After running `make bazel-build` a new binary `virtctl-4d1925c86-darwin-arm64` is created that can be executed on a Mac with an Apple Silicon processor.

steps.title: Confirm virtctl command not working on Arm Mac is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt -n kubevirt --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: This PR adds the necessary code to build and release `virtctl` for the `darwin-arm64` architecture for Macs with Apple Silicon. After running `make bazel-build` a new binary `virtctl-4d1925c86-darwin-arm64` is created that can be executed on a Mac with an Apple Silicon processor.
codeSnippets:
VERSION=$(kubectl get kubevirt.kubevirt.io/kubevirt -n kubevirt -o=jsonpath="{.status.observedKubeVirtVersion}")
# Force AMD64 build and use the emulation
ARCH=darwin-amd64
curl -L -o virtctl https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${VERSION}/virtctl-${VERSION}-${ARCH}
chmod +x virtctl
sudo install virtctl /usr/local/bin

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
